title: weinmannia laurina var. laurina
intro: Weinmannia laurina var. laurina, also known as the New Zealand honeysuckle or tī kōuka, is a variety of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, Weinmannia laurina var. laurina is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, and is also used in traditional Māori medicine to treat a range of ailments.
